_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Total Hip Replacement – Revision of Failed Hemi Arthroplasty into THR
procedure_name: Revision of failed hemi arthroplasty into THR
source_files: Revision of Failed Hemi Arthroplasty into THR_Medco.pdf
Revision of Failed Hemi Arthroplasty into THR_PPD.CPD.pdf
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 5 years of experience

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right)- confirming the diagnosis

ppd_preauth.q: Were clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings and indication for procedure submitted?
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right)- confirming the diagnosis submitted?
Were the clinical notes and x-ray report submitted are indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Was the invoice/barcode of implant submitted?
Were the clinical notes and x-ray report submitted are indicative of procedure being done?

common_queries: Clinical notes and/or X-ray report are not indicative of the procedure.
Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, or indication for procedure are missing or incomplete.
X-ray not labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right), or does not confirm the diagnosis.
Post-procedure imaging study (X-ray) is missing or incomplete.
Detailed procedure/operative note is missing or incomplete.
Invoice or barcode of the implant is missing.

clinical_key_pointers: There are various conditions in which a revision of failed hemi arthroplasty into total hip replacement is being done.
Indication: Acetabular loosening, femoral loosening and polyethylene wear etc
Diagnosis: X-ray/CT scan can be used to confirm the diagnosis.
surgical_options: The standard of care for surgical management of failed hemiarthroplasty is conversion to a total hip arthroplasty.
In case of infection two stage procedure is followed.
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: Preoperative clinical, radiographic, and laboratory assessments are critical in determining the etiology of the painful hemiarthroplasty. Establishing the etiology preoperatively is essential to plan before treating the failed hemiarthroplasty.
